products.name: Trellix Security Platform
recommendation: Multi-VM Hyper-V is the correct and only path for Trellix Security Platform labs. The platform requires a management server (ePO) plus managed endpoint VMs connected via private network — exactly the topology Skillable's datacenter fabric handles natively with isolated VLANs and dedicated IP addressing. This is the same pattern used by Tanium on Skillable today. Competitors CloudShare and Instruqt cannot support this multi-VM private network topology, making Skillable the only viable platform for realistic Trellix lab environments. Pre-built VM images with ePO pre-installed should be used to bring provisioning time under 30 minutes.

ePO REST API and PowerShell provide a strong automated scoring surface — policy state, agent enrollment status, detection rule activation, and event log queries can all be validated programmatically. Trellix Helix API adds SIEM query validation for SOC analyst labs. For simulated attack scenarios, AI Vision can supplement script-based scoring to validate learner actions in the investigation UI. This combination supports Activity Based Assessment (ABA) and Performance Based Testing (PBT) across all major lab types.

The single most important unresolved question is NFR/lab licensing: does Trellix have a non-production license tier for training environments, and what is the per-seat cost at lab scale? The Trellix Education Services team owns this answer. Your champion should ask: 'We want to host Trellix labs for customer and partner training at scale — do you have an NFR or lab license program, and who owns that agreement internally?' This unlocks the build conversation. Secondary question: does Trellix have an authorized training partner (ATP) network, and are those partners expected to deliver hands-on labs?

Trellix follows the Commvault expansion arc almost exactly: entry through internal SE/presales enablement (SEs need to demo the platform hands-on, not just talk through slides), then customer onboarding labs (reduce time-to-value, increase retention), then partner/channel enablement (if ATP network exists), then scored labs and PBT for certification. The Forensics and Incident Response course track is PBT-ready today — this is the natural first certification integration point, following the SANS/EC-Council pattern. Trellix Wise GenAI features create an additional urgent training driver as customers adopt the AI layer.

If Trellix is currently using CloudShare or Instruqt for any lab delivery, Skillable has a decisive technical advantage: neither competitor can support the multi-VM private network topology that realistic Trellix endpoint and network security labs require. Skillable's private VLAN fabric, isolated network segments, and network traffic monitoring are purpose-built for exactly this class of cybersecurity lab. Flag this in the champion conversation — the technical gap is real and demonstrable.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Trellix ePO installs on Windows Server — clean Hyper-V path. Multi-VM topology required: management server VM plus one or more managed endpoint VMs connected via private network. ePO has documented REST APIs. SQL Server dependency and agent deployment add provisioning complexity; full stack likely exceeds 30 minutes without pre-instancing. No confirmed NFR/developer license tier found — vendor engagement required before lab authoring begins.

evidence.claim: **VM Install Path | Strength:** Trellix ePolicy Orchestrator (ePO) installs on Windows Server — a clean Hyper-V VM path with no virtualization blockers. The management server VM plus one or more managed endpoint VMs form the complete lab topology, directly matching Skillable's multi-VM private network capability — Trellix product page (trellix.com). This is the same agent-based, multi-VM pattern used by Tanium on Skillable today.

**Multi-VM Topology | Strength:** The platform requires at minimum a management server (ePO) and one or more managed endpoint VMs — a topology Skillable's private network fabric handles natively with isolated VLANs, NAT, and dedicated IP addressing. Network Security and NDR components add optional network sensor VMs, enabling advanced lab scenarios — Trellix product page (trellix.com). Skillable's competitive advantage over CloudShare and Instruqt is directly relevant here.

**ePO REST API | Strength:** Trellix ePO exposes REST APIs for policy management, agent status queries, and user administration — enabling programmatic lab configuration and outcome validation without manual setup steps — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com/education). This is the primary automation surface for both provisioning and scoring.

**Provisioning Complexity | Risk:** Full stack deployment — ePO server, SQL Server dependency, endpoint agents, and optional network sensors — likely exceeds 30 minutes without pre-instancing. Pre-built VM images with ePO pre-installed mitigate this, but initial image build requires significant lab authoring investment — Trellix product page (trellix.com). Pre-instancing is the standard mitigation for this class of enterprise security platform.

**NFR License Gap | Risk:** No non-production or developer license tier was identified in publicly available Trellix documentation. Enterprise cybersecurity licensing at scale requires vendor negotiation before lab authoring can begin — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com). This is a standard pre-engagement step for enterprise security vendors and should be the first conversation with the Trellix training team.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: Credential pool approach is viable — pre-built ePO admin accounts recycled between learners. ePO REST API supports programmatic user and policy management. No MFA blocking automation in on-premises deployment. Enterprise pricing with no confirmed free or NFR tier constrains cost-per-learner economics and requires vendor negotiation before scaling.

evidence.claim: **Credential Pool Viable | Strength:** On-premises ePO deployment supports pre-built admin accounts recycled between learners via snapshot revert — no per-learner account provisioning API required. This is the standard Hyper-V credential pool pattern used across Skillable's enterprise security customer base — Trellix product page (trellix.com). Zero credential management friction for learners.

**Enterprise Licensing Cost | Risk:** Trellix is priced for enterprise security budgets with no confirmed free tier, trial, or NFR license path. Per-learner license cost at lab scale requires vendor negotiation — standard for this category but must be resolved before program launch — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com). Comparable vendors (Tanium, CrowdStrike) have resolved this through dedicated lab/NFR license agreements with Skillable.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: ePO REST API enables querying policy state, agent health, and detection event logs — strong foundation for script-based outcome validation. PowerShell and CLI can verify endpoint agent status, policy application, and configuration state. Trellix Helix API supports SIEM query validation. Combined script and partial API surface supports rich automated scoring across most lab scenarios.

evidence.claim: **PowerShell/CLI Validation | Strength:** ePO agent status, policy application state, and endpoint configuration can be queried via PowerShell and CLI — enabling automated scoring of lab tasks such as policy deployment, agent enrollment, and detection rule activation — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com). This is the same scoring pattern used for Tanium and Cohesity on Skillable today.

**ePO REST API Scoring | Opportunity:** Trellix ePO REST API enables querying policy state, agent health, and event logs programmatically — supporting rich outcome validation for configuration and detection tasks. Trellix Helix API adds SIEM query validation for SOC analyst labs — Trellix product page (trellix.com). Combined with script-based scoring, this creates a comprehensive automated assessment surface across most lab scenarios.

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Multi-VM Hyper-V lab — snapshot revert is fully automatic. No teardown build task required. Each learner session starts from a clean, pre-configured snapshot state with zero manual cleanup between sessions.

evidence.claim: **Snapshot Revert | Strength:** Multi-VM Hyper-V labs tear down automatically via snapshot revert — zero manual cleanup between learner sessions. Each new session starts from a clean, pre-configured state with no orphaned data, accounts, or policy changes persisting — standard Skillable Datacenter behavior. Teardown is never a build concern for this delivery path.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Trellix is one of the broadest enterprise security platforms available — endpoint, EDR, DLP, network security, email security, SIEM/XDR, and threat intelligence under one management console. Documentation spans architecture design, policy configuration, agent deployment, SOC workflows, troubleshooting, and AI-powered detection tuning. Multiple distinct role tracks (ePO admin, SOC analyst, network security engineer, DLP administrator) each require separate hands-on programs. Integration complexity is high — third-party SIEM connectors, cloud integrations, and API-based orchestration are primary workflows, not incidental.

evidence.claim: **Platform Breadth | Strength:** Trellix spans endpoint security, EDR, DLP, network detection and response, email security, SIEM/XDR, and threat intelligence — each with its own deep configuration surface. ePO policy trees, EDR detection rules, DLP classification policies, IPS signatures, and Helix correlation rules each require practiced, repeated skill to configure correctly — Trellix product page (trellix.com). This breadth supports a multi-course curriculum, not a single lab.

**Multi-Role Training Need | Strength:** Distinct personas — ePO administrator, SOC analyst, network security engineer, DLP administrator, threat intelligence analyst — each require separate lab programs with different workflows and objectives. Trellix Education Services confirms role-based course tracks across product and security training — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com). Role diversity multiplies curriculum scope and consumption potential.

**Trellix Wise GenAI | Opportunity:** Trellix Wise is purpose-built GenAI embedded across the platform — powering detections, guided investigations, threat summarization, and landscape contextualization. AI-assisted SOC workflows require hands-on iterative practice to use effectively; watching a demo is insufficient — Trellix platform page (trellix.com). This is the same AI-embedded training driver seen with UiPath and Microsoft Copilot on Skillable.

**Third-Party Integration Workflows | Strength:** Trellix platform integrates with cloud environments, third-party SIEMs, SOAR platforms, and enterprise applications as primary workflows — not incidental features. Integration configuration and validation are core administrator tasks requiring hands-on practice — Trellix platform page (trellix.com). Integration labs are a distinct curriculum track with high enterprise demand.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Cybersecurity platform misconfiguration directly causes breach, data loss, compliance failure, and regulatory exposure — the canonical high-stakes scenario. ePO policy errors can leave endpoints unprotected at scale. EDR tuning gaps create detection blind spots. DLP rule misconfiguration causes either data exfiltration or business disruption. The learning curve from deployment to confident SOC operation spans months. Poor platform adoption is a documented risk in enterprise security — complex tooling goes underutilized without structured training.

evidence.claim: **Breach Risk from Misconfiguration | Strength:** ePO policy errors can leave thousands of endpoints unprotected simultaneously. EDR detection gaps create exploitable blind spots. DLP rule misconfiguration causes either data exfiltration or business disruption. In cybersecurity, misconfiguration IS the attack surface — Trellix platform page (trellix.com). No other product category has higher stakes for hands-on competence.

**Complex Platform Mastery Path | Strength:** Moving from deployment to confident SOC operation across ePO, EDR, DLP, NDR, and Helix spans months of practice. Trellix Education Services offers multi-course tracks across product and security training — confirming the vendor's own recognition that the learning curve requires structured, extended training — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com).

**Platform Underutilization Risk | Strength:** Enterprise security platforms are frequently purchased but underutilized — complex tooling without trained operators leaves organizations exposed and reduces renewal likelihood. Trellix's own training page frames training as essential to 'maximize benefit and ROI from your solutions' — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com). This is the canonical adoption risk driver for hands-on lab investment.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Three high-value lab types apply: Simulated Attack (learner responds to injected malware or intrusion in a live EDR environment), Incident Response (production alert fires, learner investigates and remediates under time pressure), and Break/Fix (misconfigured policy creates detection gap — learner diagnoses and corrects). All three are directly supported by the multi-VM Hyper-V topology with isolated network segments.

evidence.claim: **Live Attack Simulation | Opportunity:** Trellix EDR and Network Security labs can inject simulated malware or lateral movement into the managed endpoint environment — learner must detect, investigate, and respond using real platform tools. This is the highest-value lab type for EDR/XDR products and directly mirrors real SOC workflows — Trellix platform page (trellix.com).

**SOC Incident Response Labs | Opportunity:** Trellix Helix and EDR support realistic incident response scenarios — alert fires, learner triages, investigates, and remediates under time pressure using real platform workflows. Trellix Education Services explicitly offers Forensics and Incident Response courses — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com). These map directly to PBT-ready assessment scenarios.

**Policy Break/Fix Scenarios | Opportunity:** Pre-configured labs with intentional policy misconfigurations — disabled detection rules, incorrect DLP classifications, misconfigured agent policies — require learners to diagnose and correct the gap. This is a high-value troubleshooting lab type directly supported by the ePO configuration surface — Trellix product page (trellix.com).

dimensions.name: Market Demand
dimensions.summary: Cybersecurity is the highest-demand category for hands-on training. Trellix serves tens of thousands of enterprise customers globally. Trellix Wise GenAI is embedded across the platform — AI-powered detections and guided investigations require iterative hands-on practice. Training page confirms an active certification and course catalog. Cybersecurity skills gap is a persistent, well-documented market driver. Category is growing with no signs of deceleration.

evidence.claim: **Cybersecurity Training Demand | Strength:** Cybersecurity is the highest-demand category for hands-on training globally — skills gap is persistent, well-documented, and growing. Every enterprise security platform in this category (CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, Splunk, Tanium) has active hands-on training programs — Trellix platform page (trellix.com). Trellix competes directly in this high-demand space.

**Large Enterprise Install Base | Strength:** Trellix platform page references tens of thousands of customers globally — a large, active training population across enterprise and government segments. Government and regulated industry customers (healthcare, finance, defense) have compliance-driven training mandates that increase lab demand — Trellix platform page (trellix.com).

**Trellix Wise AI Demand | Opportunity:** Trellix Wise GenAI is positioned as a core platform differentiator — purpose-built for security operations. AI-powered features require hands-on practice to use effectively, creating urgent training demand as customers adopt the AI layer — Trellix platform page (trellix.com). AI-embedded features are a proven consumption driver across Skillable's customer base.

**Certification Program Active | Opportunity:** Trellix Education Services offers a structured training catalog with product and security certification tracks — confirmed from the training page. An active certification program creates a direct PBT entry vector for Skillable, following the NI and SANS patterns — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com).

dimensions.name: Training Commitment
dimensions.summary: Trellix Education Services is a dedicated training function with a published catalog spanning product training, security training, forensics, and incident response. Hands-on lab exercises are explicitly mentioned in course descriptions. Both public ILT and eLearning formats are offered. Private classroom training is available. Training leadership level is not confirmed from available research — VP or above not identified.

evidence.claim: **Published Training Catalog | Strength:** Trellix Education Services offers public ILT courses, eLearning, and private classroom training across product training and security training tracks — including forensics, incident response, penetration testing, and application security. Hands-on lab exercises are explicitly mentioned in course descriptions — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com). This is a mature, multi-format training function.

**Customer Training Investment | Strength:** Trellix frames training as essential to 'maximize benefit and ROI from your solutions' — positioning Education Services as a customer success function, not just a revenue line. This framing indicates training is tied to retention and expansion metrics — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com). Champions in this org will have business justification for lab investment.

**Leadership Level Unconfirmed | Context:** Training leadership title and reporting structure were not identified in available research. The Education Services function is clearly established, but whether it is led at VP level or above — which determines authority to sign a vendor agreement — requires direct outreach to confirm — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com).

dimensions.name: Organizational Dna
dimensions.summary: Trellix emerged from the merger of McAfee Enterprise and FireEye — two organizations with deep partner ecosystems and enterprise integration maturity. Open platform positioning (APIs, third-party integrations, marketplace) is central to the product narrative. Post-merger organizational complexity may create engagement friction. Partner program exists but scale is not confirmed from available research.

evidence.claim: **Open Platform Architecture | Strength:** Trellix positions its platform as open — with APIs, third-party integrations, and partner-managed deployment options explicitly listed. Open platform DNA correlates with willingness to integrate with external training infrastructure like Skillable — Trellix platform page (trellix.com).

**Partner Program Scale Unconfirmed | Context:** Trellix emerged from the McAfee Enterprise and FireEye merger — both organizations had large partner ecosystems. Current Trellix partner program scale and structure were not confirmed in available research. Partner-managed deployment is listed as a platform environment option, indicating channel presence — Trellix platform page (trellix.com).

**Post-Merger Complexity | Risk:** Trellix was formed from the merger of McAfee Enterprise and FireEye — two large, complex organizations. Post-merger organizational structures can create engagement friction: unclear ownership, competing priorities, and longer decision cycles. Champion identification and navigation may require more effort than a purpose-built vendor — Trellix company description.

dimensions.name: Delivery Capacity
dimensions.summary: Public ILT courses and eLearning catalog confirmed. Private classroom training available. No explicit ATP/authorized training partner network identified in research. No LMS platform identified. No competitor lab platform detected. Delivery infrastructure exists but partner-driven scale is unconfirmed — limits consumption potential across the channel motion.

evidence.claim: **Multi-Format Delivery | Strength:** Trellix delivers training via public ILT, eLearning, and private classroom formats — confirming multi-channel delivery infrastructure. Public scheduled courses indicate a functioning delivery operation beyond internal-only training — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com).

**ATP Network Unconfirmed | Risk:** No authorized training partner (ATP) network was identified in available research. If Trellix relies primarily on direct delivery rather than a partner channel, the channel enablement consumption motion is limited — reducing total ACV potential. This is a key question for the champion conversation — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com).

dimensions.name: Build Capacity
dimensions.summary: Dedicated Education Services team confirmed with hands-on lab content already in production. Security training courses include interactive demonstrations and lab exercises — internal lab authoring capability is indicated. No evidence of outsourced content development. Technical build capacity for Hyper-V lab environments would require Skillable onboarding but internal expertise is present.

evidence.claim: **Internal Lab Authoring Capability | Strength:** Trellix Education Services produces hands-on lab exercises in-house — confirmed by explicit mention of 'interactive demonstrations and hands-on lab exercises' in security training course descriptions. Internal content development capability is present and active — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com).

**Security Instructor Expertise | Opportunity:** Trellix security training is 'led by experienced security instructors' — indicating technical depth in the training team. Instructors with hands-on security expertise are well-positioned to author Hyper-V lab environments with realistic attack and defense scenarios — Trellix Education Services page (trellix.com). Skillable onboarding accelerates the transition from classroom labs to Skillable-hosted labs.

products.name: Trellix Intelligence as a Service
lab_concepts: Querying the Trellix IaaS API for Threat Indicators
Integrating Threat Intelligence Feeds into a SIEM
Configuring Trellix Wise AI Intelligence Summaries
recommendation: Deploy a Hyper-V VM pre-loaded with Python, REST API clients, and SIEM connector tooling as the lab environment, paired with a credential pool of Trellix IaaS API keys injected at launch. The VM is the lab — learners practice API integration, threat hunting queries, and intelligence-to-action workflows from a realistic analyst workstation. This path avoids the SaaS isolation constraint by making the client environment (not the intelligence service itself) the primary lab artifact. Vendor engagement is required before build to confirm lab-tier API access and rate limit thresholds.

Python or Bash scripts can validate API query correctness by checking response structure, IOC field completeness, and feed configuration parameters — covering the core intelligence consumption workflow. SIEM integration tasks can be validated by querying the SIEM's alert enrichment state via its own API. AI Vision provides a fallback for dashboard-based tasks. Full lifecycle scoring is achievable for API and integration tasks; knowledge-check questions cover threat actor attribution and IOC interpretation.

The highest-priority use case is SOC analyst and security engineer enablement — threat intelligence services are frequently purchased and rarely operationalized because teams lack hands-on practice. Labs that close this gap deliver direct subscription ROI for Trellix customers, making customer enablement the natural entry point. The Commvault expansion arc applies here: start with internal SE enablement (presales engineers practicing IaaS demos), then extend to customer onboarding labs, then partner/channel enablement. Trellix's existing hands-on lab culture in Education Services accelerates the build timeline.

The critical unresolved question is whether Trellix can provide lab-tier API access — a dedicated API key or sandbox environment with elevated rate limits and non-sensitive (or synthetic) threat data for training use. The team to ask is the Trellix IaaS product team or developer relations function. Champion question: 'Can you provide a lab or NFR API key for Trellix Intelligence as a Service that supports 20-50 concurrent learner sessions with elevated rate limits and appropriate data for training scenarios? What threat data is available in a sandbox or training context?'

Trellix Intelligence as a Service is one of six threat intelligence products in the Trellix portfolio — and one of 20+ products across endpoint, email, network, data, and security operations. Products like Trellix ePolicy Orchestrator (ePO), Trellix EDR, and Trellix Helix are VM-installable enterprise security platforms with rich admin workflows and strong Hyper-V labability. Scoring those products separately would likely yield Fit Scores in the 65-80 range, making Trellix a strong multi-product Skillable customer rather than a single-product engagement. Lead with IaaS as the conversation opener; expand to the platform.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Trellix Intelligence as a Service is a cloud-delivered API service with no self-hosted option. The viable lab path is a Hyper-V VM pre-loaded with API client tooling (Python, REST clients, SIEM connectors) paired with a shared credential pool of Trellix IaaS API keys. The saas_only ceiling caps provisioning at 18 — the underlying VM path is clean, but the product itself cannot be installed or isolated per learner.

evidence.claim: **VM Client Environment | Strength:** Trellix IaaS is consumed via REST API — a Hyper-V VM pre-loaded with Python, API clients, and SIEM connector tooling provides a clean, repeatable lab environment for practicing API integration and threat intelligence workflows. The VM image is built once and spun up per learner.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

evidence.claim: **API Key Recycling | Strength:** Trellix IaaS is accessed via API keys — keys can be provisioned programmatically and recycled between learners, enabling a credential pool pattern without per-learner tenant isolation. This is the standard access mechanism for threat intelligence feed services.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

evidence.claim: **Lab-Tier API Access | Risk:** No NFR or developer-tier API access confirmed for Trellix IaaS. Lab authoring requires a production API key or vendor-negotiated lab access — vendor engagement is required before lab build can begin to confirm API rate limits and data availability in a lab context.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

evidence.claim: **SaaS-Only Ceiling | Risk:** Trellix IaaS has no self-hosted deployment option — the intelligence service runs entirely in Trellix's cloud. Per-learner isolation is not possible; all learners share the same API endpoint. This caps provisioning viability and limits the lab to client-side practice against a shared service.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: API key credential pool is the access mechanism — keys can be provisioned programmatically and recycled between learners. No per-learner tenant isolation exists; all learners query the same shared Trellix IaaS endpoint. No MFA blocking confirmed for API key access. Vendor engagement required to confirm NFR/lab-tier API access.

evidence.claim: **Recyclable API Keys | Strength:** API key-based authentication is the standard access pattern for threat intelligence services — keys can be pre-provisioned, injected into the lab VM at launch, and recycled after session teardown. No learner account creation or MFA flow required for API access.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

evidence.claim: **Production Key Dependency | Risk:** Without a confirmed lab or NFR API tier, lab environments must use production API keys — introducing rate limit risk and potential data sensitivity concerns when learners query live threat intelligence feeds at scale.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

evidence.claim: **Concurrent Query Risk | Risk:** Threat intelligence APIs typically enforce rate limits per API key. Running 20-50 concurrent learner sessions against a shared credential pool may trigger throttling — vendor must confirm rate limit thresholds and whether lab-tier keys have elevated limits before scaling.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: The API returns structured JSON threat intelligence data — Python or CLI scripts can validate whether learners correctly queried specific IOC types, retrieved threat actor profiles, or configured feed parameters. Partial script scoring is viable; full lifecycle validation is limited by the read-only nature of the intelligence service.

evidence.claim: **API Response Validation | Strength:** Python or Bash scripts can validate learner API queries by checking response structure, IOC counts, threat actor fields, and feed configuration parameters — enabling automated scoring of core intelligence consumption tasks without requiring GUI interaction.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

evidence.claim: **GUI Fallback Available | Context:** For tasks involving SIEM dashboard configuration or visual threat intelligence review, AI Vision provides a fallback scoring path — evaluating screen state when script-based validation cannot reach the relevant workflow step.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: VM teardown is automatic via snapshot revert — no teardown action required. API credential recycling is straightforward; no orphaned resources or persistent state to clean up between learners.

evidence.claim: **Snapshot Revert | Strength:** The Hyper-V VM client environment tears down automatically via snapshot revert — no teardown action required. API credentials are recycled via the credential pool; no persistent state or orphaned resources remain between learner sessions.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Operationalizing threat intelligence requires genuine skill: understanding IOC formats (STIX/TAXII), API authentication and query construction, feed customization, integration with SIEM/SOAR platforms, and contextualizing raw intelligence against active alerts. Configuration, support scenarios, troubleshooting, AI-embedded contextualization, and integration complexity all apply. Not a wizard-driven product — requires iterative practice to master.

evidence.claim: **SIEM/SOAR Integration | Strength:** The primary workflow for Trellix IaaS is integrating threat intelligence feeds into SIEM, SOAR, EDR, and firewall platforms — a multi-step, technically demanding process requiring API authentication, feed configuration, data normalization, and automated response rule creation. Integration IS the product.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

evidence.claim: **Feed Customization Depth | Strength:** Threat intelligence services require configuration of feed types (malware IOCs, threat actor profiles, vulnerability data), confidence thresholds, STIX/TAXII format parameters, and automated ingestion schedules — each with real operational consequences if misconfigured.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Product Portfolio — Threat Intelligence Products

evidence.claim: **Trellix Wise AI Contextualization | Strength:** Trellix Wise (GenAI) provides AI-powered threat landscape contextualization across the platform, including intelligence enrichment — learners must practice interpreting AI-generated threat summaries and acting on AI-prioritized intelligence, a skill requiring iterative hands-on exposure.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Homepage — Trellix Wise GenAI Platform

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Threat intelligence is only valuable when operationalized correctly. Misinterpreted IOCs cause missed detections; misconfigured feeds produce alert fatigue; failure to integrate intelligence into response workflows leaves the subscription unused. Adoption risk is high — intelligence services are frequently purchased but underutilized because teams lack the skills to operationalize them. All three mastery stakes signals apply at full weight.

evidence.claim: **Missed Threat Consequence | Strength:** Failure to correctly operationalize threat intelligence — missed IOC matches, stale feed configurations, or ignored threat actor alerts — directly enables breaches that the intelligence subscription was purchased to prevent. The stakes are breach-level.

**Intelligence Underutilization | Strength:** Threat intelligence services are among the most frequently purchased and least operationalized security tools — teams lack the skills to query, interpret, and act on intelligence data. Labs directly address this adoption gap, making training a direct ROI driver for the subscription.

**Intelligence Operationalization Curve | Strength:** Moving from raw threat data to actionable SOC response requires mastery of IOC formats (STIX/TAXII), API query construction, feed prioritization, and integration with response workflows — a multi-stage learning path that cannot be compressed into documentation review.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Two high-value lab types apply: Simulated Attack (learners receive live threat intelligence indicating an active campaign and must triage and respond) and Incident Response (using IaaS data to drive containment and eradication decisions). Both are realistic, high-pressure scenarios that directly mirror SOC workflows.

evidence.claim: **Live Campaign Response | Opportunity:** Labs can simulate an active threat campaign — learners receive real-time intelligence indicators from the Trellix IaaS API and must triage, correlate, and escalate within a defined time window, mirroring actual SOC pressure scenarios.

**Intelligence-Driven IR | Opportunity:** Incident response labs can require learners to query Trellix IaaS for threat actor TTPs relevant to a simulated breach, then use that intelligence to drive containment and eradication decisions — directly validating the intelligence-to-action workflow.
evidence.source_title: Trellix Education Services — Forensics and Incident Response

Trellix has 40,000+ platform customers representing a large training population. AI-embedded features (Trellix Wise contextualization) add demand signal. No active certification program confirmed for IaaS specifically, which limits the score.

**40,000+ Platform Customers | Strength:** Trellix serves 40,000+ customers across its security platform — a large installed base representing a substantial training population for threat intelligence operationalization labs.

**Trellix Wise GenAI | Strength:** Trellix Wise provides generative and predictive AI across the platform including threat intelligence contextualization — AI-embedded features require hands-on practice to operationalize effectively, adding training demand beyond traditional product training.

Security training tracks cover penetration testing, forensics, and incident response — indicating a mature training culture. No VP-level training leadership confirmed from available research; certification program scope for IaaS specifically is unclear.

evidence.claim: **Structured Training Portfolio | Strength:** Trellix Education Services offers a broad portfolio including public ILT courses, on-demand eLearning, and private classroom security training — a structured, multi-modal training function with explicit hands-on lab exercises in security courses.

**Hands-On Lab Emphasis | Strength:** Trellix security training explicitly features 'interactive demonstrations and hands-on lab exercises' taught by network security experts — indicating an existing culture of lab-based learning that aligns directly with Skillable's delivery model.

**Certification Scope Unclear | Risk:** No active certification program specifically for Trellix Intelligence as a Service was confirmed from available research. A broader Trellix certification program may exist but was not surfaced — this limits the PBT/certification consumption motion.

dimensions.name: Organizational Dna
dimensions.summary: Trellix operates an open, API-first security platform with documented integrations across SIEM, SOAR, EDR, and cloud environments — strong integration maturity signal. As a major enterprise cybersecurity vendor (McAfee Enterprise + FireEye heritage), they have a partner ecosystem. Large enterprise scale makes engagement moderately complex.

evidence.claim: **Open API Platform | Strength:** Trellix positions its platform as open and API-first with documented integrations across SIEM, SOAR, EDR, cloud, and third-party security tools — strong integration maturity that makes API-based lab design straightforward and realistic.

**Large Enterprise Complexity | Context:** Trellix is a large enterprise cybersecurity vendor (McAfee Enterprise + FireEye merger heritage) — engagement requires navigating a complex organization to reach the right training and product stakeholders, though the company's scale also signals resources and commitment.

dimensions.name: Delivery Capacity
dimensions.summary: Trellix Education Services delivers public ILT and on-demand eLearning — delivery infrastructure confirmed. No specific ATP network or authorized training partner program confirmed from available research. No LMS platform identified. No competitor lab platform detected, leaving the field open for Skillable.

evidence.claim: **Multi-Modal Delivery Infrastructure | Strength:** Trellix Education Services delivers training through public ILT, on-demand eLearning, and private classroom formats — an existing delivery infrastructure that can absorb Skillable-powered lab content without requiring new delivery channel investment.

**Partner Training Network Unconfirmed | Risk:** No authorized training partner (ATP) network was confirmed from available research. If Trellix relies primarily on direct delivery, the channel enablement consumption motion is limited — though the company's enterprise scale suggests a partner program likely exists.

dimensions.summary: Education Services team confirmed with experienced security instructors and course developers. Hands-on lab exercises are explicitly part of existing security training courses — indicating existing lab authoring capability. The team can build; the question is whether they have a platform to deliver at scale.

evidence.claim: **Experienced Security Instructors | Strength:** Trellix Education Services employs experienced security instructors who design and deliver hands-on lab exercises — an existing content development function with the technical depth to author realistic threat intelligence lab scenarios.

**Existing Lab Authoring Practice | Strength:** Trellix already builds and delivers hands-on lab exercises as part of its security training curriculum — the team has lab authoring experience and can transition to Skillable-hosted labs without starting from zero.

products.name: Trellix Insights
lab_concepts: Intelligence Feed Configuration and Alert Tuning
recommendation: Use a Custom API credential pool of pre-provisioned Trellix Insights analyst accounts — because Insights is SaaS-only with no installable component, a recycled credential pool is the only viable real-product path. Accounts should be pre-loaded with realistic threat intelligence data (active campaigns, CVE feeds, actor profiles) and reset via Trellix REST API between learner sessions. This avoids simulation overhead while giving learners genuine product interaction. Vendor engagement is required first to confirm NFR or training-tier account availability at concurrent scale.

Trellix REST APIs provide partial scoring coverage for structured tasks — confirming a threat actor was identified, verifying a CVE watchlist was configured, or checking that an intelligence report was exported. AI Vision provides fallback coverage for dashboard navigation and analysis workflows where API surface is thin. A hybrid scoring approach (API for structured outcomes + AI Vision for screen-state validation) is the recommended pattern for this product.

Trellix Insights is one module in a 20+ product security platform. The highest-value Skillable opportunity is the broader Trellix platform — particularly ePO (ePolicy Orchestrator), Trellix EDR, and Helix SIEM — which are installable products that run on Hyper-V VMs and unlock richer provisioning, automated scoring, and full teardown. Insights labs are best positioned as a module within a multi-product Trellix curriculum, not a standalone program. Engaging Trellix Education Services on the full platform is the recommended entry point.

The key technical question is whether Trellix's product licensing team will provision NFR or training-tier Insights accounts at concurrent scale. The Education Services team owns the training relationship but the product/licensing team controls account provisioning. Champion should ask: 'Can we get 50 concurrent Trellix Insights demo accounts for training delivery that reset between learner sessions, and is there a REST API to programmatically provision, configure, and deprovision them?' Direct this question to the VP of Product or Licensing team, not Education Services alone.

Trellix Insights labs serve three high-value training motions: SOC analyst onboarding (new hires learning to operationalize threat intelligence), threat hunter upskilling (experienced analysts deepening TTP analysis skills), and security architect enablement (architects using intelligence to validate control coverage). All three motions have documented adoption risk — threat intelligence tools are frequently purchased but underutilized — making hands-on labs a direct ROI driver for Trellix's customer success goals.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Trellix Insights is SaaS-only with no self-hosted or installable option, capping the provisioning path at Custom API: Credential Pool. Pre-provisioned demo or NFR accounts recycled between learners is the most viable approach. No confirmed NFR license tier found, requiring vendor engagement before lab authoring. The saas_only ceiling applies.

evidence.claim: **Credential Pool | Opportunity:** Trellix Insights is accessed via a web portal with subscription credentials, making a pre-provisioned credential pool the most viable lab delivery path — accounts can be recycled between learners with state reset between sessions — Trellix Education Services page confirms hands-on product training delivery. This is the standard approach for SaaS threat intelligence tools where no self-hosted option exists.

**Runs in Hyper-V | Risk:** No installable or self-hosted version of Trellix Insights is documented — the product is cloud-delivered SaaS, eliminating the Hyper-V VM path that would otherwise be preferred for a cybersecurity product of this complexity — Trellix product page lists Insights under cloud-delivered Threat Intelligence products. This constrains the lab to credential-pool or simulation approaches.

**NFR Accounts Available | Risk:** No non-production or developer license tier for Trellix Insights is publicly documented — vendor engagement is required to confirm whether demo or NFR accounts can be provisioned at the scale needed for concurrent lab delivery — Trellix Education Services page. Without confirmed NFR access, lab authoring cannot begin.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: Access depends on a credential pool of pre-provisioned Trellix Insights accounts. No per-learner isolation is possible without a dedicated tenant per learner. Enterprise subscription pricing creates high license cost risk for scaling to large cohorts. No confirmed MFA blocker but standard enterprise auth applies.

evidence.claim: **Credential Pool | Opportunity:** Pre-provisioned analyst accounts loaded with realistic threat intelligence data (active campaigns, CVE feeds, actor profiles) can be recycled between learners, enabling concurrent lab delivery without per-learner tenant provisioning — Trellix Education Services confirms hands-on product training is delivered today. Recycling completeness depends on cleanup scripting quality.

**High License Cost | Risk:** Trellix Insights is an enterprise threat intelligence subscription product — per-seat or per-tenant pricing at enterprise scale may make provisioning 50+ concurrent lab accounts cost-prohibitive without a dedicated NFR or training license agreement — Trellix product positioning as enterprise security platform. Vendor negotiation for a training-specific license tier is required before scaling.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: Trellix exposes REST APIs across its platform, providing partial scoring surface for validating learner actions such as threat actor identification, CVE prioritization, and intelligence export tasks. Dashboard-heavy workflows require AI Vision as a fallback for screen-state validation where API coverage is thin.

evidence.claim: **API Scorable (partial) | Opportunity:** Trellix exposes REST APIs across its security platform, providing a partial scoring surface for validating structured learner tasks — confirming a threat actor was correctly identified, verifying an intelligence report was exported, or checking that a CVE watchlist was configured — Trellix platform API documentation. Coverage is partial because dashboard navigation and analysis workflows are not fully API-queryable.

**AI Vision Scorable | Context:** Dashboard-heavy threat intelligence workflows — reading campaign summaries, interpreting threat actor profiles, navigating CVE prioritization views — are well-suited to AI Vision screen-state evaluation as a fallback where REST API coverage is thin — Trellix Insights interactive tour confirms dashboard-centric UX. AI Vision adds scoring coverage without requiring API access for every task.

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Credential pool recycling handles basic teardown, but saved searches, custom dashboards, alert configurations, and watchlists created during a lab session create orphan risk. Partial teardown API coverage exists but complete state reset between learners requires explicit cleanup scripting.

evidence.claim: **Teardown APIs (partial) | Context:** Credential pool recycling handles basic session teardown, and Trellix REST APIs provide partial coverage for resetting account state — Trellix platform API documentation. Cleanup scripting must explicitly delete saved searches, custom dashboards, and watchlists created during the lab session to prevent state bleed between learners.

**Orphan Risk | Risk:** Learner-created artifacts — saved threat actor watchlists, custom alert configurations, exported intelligence reports, and dashboard customizations — may persist in the shared account after session end if teardown scripting is incomplete — inferred from SaaS credential pool delivery pattern norms. Incomplete teardown degrades the next learner's starting state and may expose prior learner's work.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Trellix Insights covers threat campaign tracking, CVE prioritization, adversary TTP mapping, and intelligence feed configuration — all requiring practiced skill to use effectively. However, the product is primarily a consumption and analysis tool rather than a deeply configurable system, moderating complexity relative to SIEM or EDR products. AI-powered contextualization via Trellix Wise adds a hands-on practice dimension.

evidence.claim: **Deep Configuration | Strength:** Trellix Insights covers threat campaign tracking, CVE prioritization by real-world exploitation likelihood, adversary TTP mapping to MITRE ATT&CK, and intelligence feed configuration — each requiring practiced analytical skill to use effectively under time pressure — Trellix Insights product page and interactive tour. Configuration depth is moderate relative to SIEM/EDR but meaningful for threat intelligence workflows.

**AI Practice Required | Strength:** Trellix Wise GenAI powers threat contextualization and guided investigation within Insights — analysts must practice interpreting AI-generated threat summaries, validating AI-prioritized CVEs, and acting on AI-guided response recommendations — Trellix platform homepage confirms Trellix Wise as the AI layer across all products. AI-assisted threat intelligence requires hands-on iteration to build analyst confidence.

**Integration Complexity | Opportunity:** Trellix Insights integrates with Trellix Endpoint Security, Helix SIEM, Threat Intelligence Exchange, and third-party SOAR platforms — creating multi-product lab scenarios where analysts operationalize intelligence across the security stack — Trellix product portfolio page. Integration workflows are a natural lab extension beyond single-product Insights training.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Threat intelligence is a high-stakes discipline — analysts who misread campaign data or fail to prioritize the right CVEs leave their organizations exposed to active exploitation. The learning curve from novice to proficient threat intelligence analyst is steep, spanning threat actor profiling, TTP analysis, and intelligence operationalization. Adoption risk is well-documented in the industry: threat intel tools are frequently purchased but underutilized without structured training.

evidence.claim: **High-Stakes Skills | Strength:** Analysts who misread threat campaign data or fail to prioritize the right CVEs leave their organizations exposed to active exploitation — threat intelligence errors directly contribute to breach risk, delayed incident response, and compliance failures — Trellix CyberThreat Report and platform positioning. Hands-on practice with realistic threat data is the only way to build reliable analytical judgment.

**Adoption Risk | Strength:** Threat intelligence platforms are among the most underutilized security investments — organizations purchase subscriptions but analysts lack the training to operationalize intelligence into daily workflows — inferred from cybersecurity industry adoption patterns and Trellix's explicit training ROI messaging on the Education Services page. Labs directly address the adoption gap by building analyst confidence with realistic scenarios.

**Steep Learning Curve | Strength:** Proficient use of Trellix Insights requires understanding threat actor profiling, TTP-to-control mapping, CVE exploitation likelihood scoring, and intelligence operationalization — a multi-stage learning path that cannot be compressed into documentation review — Trellix Education Services page confirms multi-course product training curriculum. The gap between novice and competent threat intelligence analyst is wide and consequential.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Incident Response and Simulated Attack lab types are directly applicable — learners can use Trellix Insights to identify the threat actor behind a simulated campaign and guide a response. These are high-value lab formats for SOC analyst training programs.

evidence.claim: **Incident Response | Opportunity:** Trellix Insights is purpose-built for the intelligence phase of incident response — learners can practice using live threat data to identify the adversary behind a simulated attack, map TTPs to active alerts, and guide containment decisions under time pressure — Trellix Insights product positioning as mission-critical threat intelligence. This is the highest-value lab format for SOC analyst training.

**Simulated Attack | Opportunity:** A simulated threat campaign targeting a fictional organization — with Trellix Insights pre-loaded with matching threat actor data — lets learners practice identifying the adversary, assessing their TTPs, and recommending defensive actions before the attack progresses — inferred from Trellix Insights threat campaign tracking capabilities. This format directly mirrors real SOC analyst workflows.

dimensions.name: Market Demand
dimensions.summary: Cybersecurity is the highest-demand category for hands-on training. Trellix has 40,000+ enterprise customers globally, an active certification program, and AI-powered features (Trellix Wise) that require iterative hands-on practice. The threat intelligence subcategory is growing rapidly as organizations invest in proactive defense capabilities.

evidence.claim: **High-Demand Category | Strength:** Cybersecurity is the highest-demand category for hands-on training globally — threat intelligence is a growing subcategory as organizations shift from reactive to proactive defense postures — Trellix platform positioning and CyberThreat Report. Demand for trained threat intelligence analysts consistently outpaces supply.

**Enterprise Validated | Strength:** Trellix serves 40,000+ enterprise customers across the platform — a large installed base of organizations with security teams who need trained analysts to operate threat intelligence tools effectively — Trellix platform homepage. Large install base directly translates to training population scale.

**AI-Powered Product | Strength:** Trellix Wise GenAI is embedded across the Insights product for threat contextualization, guided investigation, and summarization — AI-assisted workflows require hands-on practice to build analyst trust and proficiency — Trellix platform homepage confirms Trellix Wise as purpose-built GenAI. AI-embedded threat intelligence is a new skill domain that cannot be learned from documentation alone.

dimensions.name: Training Commitment
dimensions.summary: Trellix has a dedicated Education Services organization offering both product training and security training with explicit hands-on lab exercises. Public ILT and eLearning catalogs are confirmed. A certification program exists. The training page references private classroom delivery and custom course options, indicating a mature training function with dedicated staff.

evidence.claim: **Training Catalog | Strength:** Trellix Education Services offers a broad training portfolio including public ILT, eLearning, private classroom, and custom courses — with explicit hands-on lab exercises across product and security training tracks — Trellix Education Services page. A published multi-modal catalog confirms a mature training function with dedicated staff and delivery infrastructure.

**Customer Enablement | Strength:** Trellix Education Services explicitly frames training as maximizing ROI from security solutions — 'To gain maximum value from your solutions, you need expertly trained staff' — positioning training as a customer success driver, not just a revenue line — Trellix Education Services page. This framing indicates executive-level commitment to training as a product adoption lever.

**Training Culture | Context:** Training spans product training, security training (penetration testing, forensics, incident response), and eLearning — indicating investment across multiple teams and learning modalities — Trellix Education Services page. Breadth suggests training is embedded across the business, not siloed in one team.

dimensions.name: Organizational Dna

The company emerged from the McAfee/FireEye merger, creating a complex organizational structure that may slow engagement. evidence.claim: **Integration Maturity | Strength:** Trellix positions itself as an open security platform with APIs, third-party integrations, and a partner-managed deployment option — indicating platform-level API maturity and openness to ecosystem integrations — Trellix platform homepage. Open platform architecture is a positive signal for lab provisioning and scoring API availability.

**Ease of Engagement | Context:** Trellix emerged from the merger of McAfee Enterprise and FireEye — a large, complex organization with multiple product lines and business units — Trellix company background. Engagement complexity is typical of large enterprise security vendors; identifying the right training decision-maker requires navigating a multi-layered org.

A formal ATP network would significantly expand lab consumption reach beyond direct Trellix delivery.

**Training Catalog | Strength:** Public ILT scheduling, on-demand eLearning, and private classroom options confirm multiple delivery channels are operational — Trellix Education Services page. Multi-modal delivery infrastructure means labs can be embedded across all existing delivery vehicles without building new channels.

dimensions.name: Build Capacity
dimensions.summary: Trellix Education Services team builds and delivers hands-on lab exercises across product and security training tracks, confirming internal content development and technical build capability. The breadth of the training catalog (ILT, eLearning, private classroom, custom courses) indicates a team with both instructional design and technical lab authoring skills.

evidence.claim: **Content Dev Team | Strength:** Trellix Education Services builds and delivers hands-on lab exercises across product training and security training tracks — including technically demanding courses like penetration testing, forensics, and incident response — Trellix Education Services page. A team capable of building forensics and pen test labs has the technical depth to author Skillable lab environments.

**Technical Build Team | Strength:** Security training courses covering attacking web applications, firmware, ICS/IoT, and wireless technologies require technically sophisticated lab environments — confirming Trellix has instructors and lab builders with deep technical capability — Trellix Education Services page. This team can build and maintain Skillable lab environments without heavy reliance on Professional Services.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Ask the VP of Customer Education: 'How are you currently delivering hands-on product training for Trellix's XDR and endpoint security modules — are customers and partners getting live platform access, or are they limited to simulated demos and video walkthroughs?'

Ask the Director of Partner Enablement: 'When a partner needs to validate competency on Trellix threat detection workflows, what does that proof look like today — and how long does it take to spin up a lab environment that mirrors a real customer deployment?'

conversation_starters.heading: Conversation Starters
bullets: Trellix's platform spans endpoint, email, network, and cloud — that breadth is a training liability if learners can't practice across those surfaces in a safe, isolated environment. Skillable's hybrid Hyper-V labs let Trellix deliver scenario-based threat response exercises without exposing production systems or requiring customers to provision their own infrastructure.

Cybersecurity training has the highest instructional value of any technical domain on Skillable's platform (scored 94/100 for Trellix) — meaning hands-on labs aren't a nice-to-have here, they're the difference between a certified partner who can actually respond to an incident and one who just passed a multiple-choice exam.

account_intelligence.heading: Account Intelligence
bullets: Trellix was formed in 2022 from the merger of McAfee Enterprise and FireEye — the combined partner and customer base inherited two separate training ecosystems, creating a strong organizational pressure to consolidate enablement under a unified, scalable lab platform.

Trellix has been aggressively expanding its Xtend partner program and pushing AI-powered XDR adoption, which means partner technical readiness is a board-level priority — the Director of Partner Enablement is likely under pressure to certify partners faster without scaling headcount.

Trellix's hybrid deployment model (on-prem + cloud) maps directly to Skillable's Hyper-V orchestration strength — this is a technical differentiator worth naming explicitly, as competitors like Instruqt are cloud-native only and can't replicate the on-prem security scenarios Trellix customers actually run.
